content.text:
Now I have a very clear view of atom N:43 and its covalent bonds. From this angle, I can confirm:

**Atom N:43 (Nitrogen)** has **3 covalently bonded neighbours:**

| Atomic Index | Element |
|---|---|
| **41** | **C** (Carbon) — bonded below |
| **44** | **C** (Carbon) — bonded upper-left |
| **49** | **C** (Carbon) — bonded upper-right |

These three bonds form the connections of N:43 within the imidazole-like ring system visible in the molecule.

**geomconf_index: 8640**

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 43, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
